plan.action: HOLD - no new entries, no exits. At max positions with strong regime and no thesis breaks.
plan.watch: FIX is only red position at -2.7% but well above -7% stop. LITE recovered from yesterday (was 11.78% off high, now 8.16%). TER and APH both confirmed Super tier in today's scan - validates holdings.

input.reflection: Second consecutive hold day. Portfolio at 5/5 max, regime strong, all positions above stops. Notably, TER and APH — two of my holdings — appear as Super Stocks in today's scan, confirming the original entry thesis is intact. LITE's relative weakness (flagged yesterday as a potential swap candidate) actually improved today: moved from 11.78% off high to 8.16% off high, and P&L moved from marginal to +4.25%. This is a lesson in patience — had I force-swapped LITE yesterday based on the 'weakest holding' thesis, I would have missed today's recovery. VIX dropped to 18.71 (below yesterday's tick-up warning), so defensive posture not triggered. FIX is the only red position but -2.7% is normal noise, not a thesis break. No new rule warranted — 'don't churn at max positions' is baseline-encoded and I'm executing it. The shadow activity (9 would-buys in 5 days) suggests the screener is finding plenty of setups; the discipline is declining them when capacity is full rather than forcing swaps to chase every fresh idea.
